content:
Add a play button to each row in the _RankingSheet popup (lib/screens/users_screen.dart) so the user can play back their own voice note they recorded when liking that person.

## How voice notes are stored:
- When a like is made, a voice note is uploaded to the backend
- GET /api/like/{liked_user_id}/voice returns the audio file (or a URL to it)
- The backend serves audio at GET /api/voice/{filename}
- Base URL: http://10.0.2.2:8000

## Changes needed in _RankingSheet:
1. Add audioplayers: already in pubspec.yaml — import it
2. In _RankingSheetState, add:
   - AudioPlayer _audioPlayer = AudioPlayer()
   - Map<int, bool> _playing = {} (tracks which user ID is currently playing)
   - Map<int, String?> _voiceUrls = {} (cache of voice note URLs per user id)
   - dispose: _audioPlayer.dispose()

3. Add method _fetchVoiceUrl(int userId) → String?:
   - GET http://10.0.2.2:8000/api/like/{userId}/voice with Bearer token
   - If 200, returns the audio URL
   - If 404, returns null (no voice note)

4. Add method _togglePlay(int userId):
   - If currently playing this user: _audioPlayer.stop(), set _playing[userId]=false
   - Else: fetch URL if not cached, play with _audioPlayer.play(UrlSource(url)), set _playing[userId]=true
   - On completion: set _playing[userId]=false

5. In each row, add a small play button on the right (before the drag handle):
   - Green circle (Color(0xFF00C853)), icon: Icons.play_arrow if not playing, Icons.stop if playing
   - Size: 32x32
   - Only show if voice note exists (check _voiceUrls after fetch — hide if null)
   - On tap: _togglePlay(item.id)

Keep all existing restyle and logic intact. When done run: openclaw system event --text "Done: voice playback in ranking popup" --mode now

message.content:
Add a play button to each row in the _RankingSheet popup (lib/screens/users_screen.dart) so the user can play back their own voice note they recorded when liking that person.

## How voice notes are stored:
- When a like is made, a voice note is uploaded to the backend
- GET /api/like/{liked_user_id}/voice returns the audio file (or a URL to it)
- The backend serves audio at GET /api/voice/{filename}
- Base URL: http://10.0.2.2:8000

## Changes needed in _RankingSheet:
1. Add audioplayers: already in pubspec.yaml — import it
2. In _RankingSheetState, add:
   - AudioPlayer _audioPlayer = AudioPlayer()
   - Map<int, bool> _playing = {} (tracks which user ID is currently playing)
   - Map<int, String?> _voiceUrls = {} (cache of voice note URLs per user id)
   - dispose: _audioPlayer.dispose()

3. Add method _fetchVoiceUrl(int userId) → String?:
   - GET http://10.0.2.2:8000/api/like/{userId}/voice with Bearer token
   - If 200, returns the audio URL
   - If 404, returns null (no voice note)

4. Add method _togglePlay(int userId):
   - If currently playing this user: _audioPlayer.stop(), set _playing[userId]=false
   - Else: fetch URL if not cached, play with _audioPlayer.play(UrlSource(url)), set _playing[userId]=true
   - On completion: set _playing[userId]=false

5. In each row, add a small play button on the right (before the drag handle):
   - Green circle (Color(0xFF00C853)), icon: Icons.play_arrow if not playing, Icons.stop if playing
   - Size: 32x32
   - Only show if voice note exists (check _voiceUrls after fetch — hide if null)
   - On tap: _togglePlay(item.id)

Keep all existing restyle and logic intact. When done run: openclaw system event --text "Done: voice playback in ranking popup" --mode now

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- less-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.thinking:
The FutureBuilder approach has a problem — it will re-fetch every time the widget rebuilds because `_voiceUrls.containsKey(item.id)` changes inside setState. Actually wait, let me think more carefully.

The FutureBuilder is built with:
```dart
future: _voiceUrls.containsKey(item.id)
    ? null
    : _fetchVoiceUrl(item.id).then(...)
```

When `_voiceUrls` doesn't contain the key, it passes a future. When it does contain the key, it passes null. This will work on first build. 

On subsequent rebuilds after setState is called, `_voiceUrls.containsKey(item.id)` will be true, so it passes null instead of a new future — preventing re-fetches. But there's a subtle issue: passing `null` to FutureBuilder shows `ConnectionState.none`, which should display the cached state fine. The real problem is on the initial build — if the itemBuilder gets called multiple times before the first fetch completes, it creates a new future each time, potentially triggering multiple concurrent requests for the same item.

This isn't catastrophic since the fetches run in parallel and all set the same URL value, but there's a cleaner approach using initState to pre-populate or manage the futures differently. I'm realizing the FutureBuilder approach should actually work fine since the list uses ValueKey based on item.id, keeping the widget tree stable across rebuilds. When the future completes and setState updates `_voiceUrls`, the FutureBuilder will show the cached URL instead of waiting on a future, so the play button displays correctly.

The real concern is that if the list gets reordered or items change before a fetch completes, new futures might be created for the same item, causing duplicate concurrent requests.

I could solve this by caching the futures themselves—storing them in a map keyed by item ID so that multiple rebuilds reuse the same future instead of creating new ones.

content.text: The `FutureBuilder` inside `itemBuilder` has an anti-pattern issue (new futures on every rebuild). Let me refactor to cache futures properly:

lastPrompt: Add a play button to each row in the _RankingSheet popup (lib/screens/users_screen.dart) so the user can play back their own voice note they recorded when liking that person.  ## How voice notes are s…

content.text:
Done. Here's what was added to `_RankingSheetState`:

- `_audioPlayer`, `_playing`, `_voiceUrls`, `_voiceFutures` fields
- `dispose()` calls `_audioPlayer.dispose()`
- `onPlayerComplete` listener registered once in `initState` (clears `_playing` on completion)
- `_fetchVoiceUrl(userId)` — GETs `/api/like/{userId}/voice` with Bearer token, returns URL or null
- `_togglePlay(userId)` — stops if playing, otherwise fetches URL (if not cached) and plays
- Each row's `trailing` now has a green 32×32 circle play/stop button that only appears once the voice URL resolves as non-null; `_voiceFutures.putIfAbsent` prevents duplicate fetches across rebuilds
